mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during low-rate periods are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and real new house sales confirms broad-based aggregate demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive forward equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 55% of quarters where both series expand, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive value over GDP alone (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects improving manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and triggers positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when inflation expectations exceed 3%, or Rank IC remains negative during periods of capacity utilization above 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation print exceeding 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets post positive returns in more than 60% of months following inflation prints above 0.5%, or the signal loses significance when real yields remain negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year sample, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with positive payroll revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in housing starts and real new house sales signal a sharp contraction in residential investment demand, which drags on broader economic activity and produces negative forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55% of months where both housing indicators decline, or the joint housing signal shows no statistical edge over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization signals supply-side bottlenecks that constrain output growth, leading to margin compression and negative forward equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters where industrial production expands alongside capacity utilization above 82%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
